question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted :fileId value (e.g., a non-standard address book file ID other than 102) to the GET /api/v1/network/nodes endpoint, causing the latest_address_book CTE to select a historical or alternate address book version, thereby presenting a reorganized view of network node history to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly query with different :fileId values (e.g., file IDs 101 and 102) to enumerate all historical address book snapshots, reconstructing a full timeline of node additions and removals that was never intended to be exposed as a unified history view?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_address_book CTE's ORDER BY start_consensus_timestamp DESC LIMIT 1 logic by querying with a fileId that maps to an address book with a manipulated or injected start_consensus_timestamp, causing a stale address book to be selected as the 'latest', effectively reorganizing the visible node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a :minNodeId and :maxNodeId range that, combined with a specific :fileId, selects address book entries from a historical snapshot rather than the current one, causing the API to return a reorganized view of which nodes were active at a past consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass :orderDirection as a value that causes the CASE-based ORDER BY (case when :orderDirection = 'ASC' then abe.node_id end asc) to produce a non-deterministic or unexpected ordering of node records, allowing the attacker to present network nodes in an order that misrepresents the historical sequence of node additions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an :orderDirection value that is neither 'ASC' nor 'DESC' (e.g., an empty string or unexpected token), causing both CASE branches to evaluate to NULL and producing an undefined/implementation-dependent sort order that reorganizes the returned node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_node_stake CTE's subquery (select max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake) to observe that stale stake data is joined to current address book entries, causing the API to return a historically inconsistent view of node stake assignments that misrepresents the current network state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition by issuing concurrent requests during an address book update, where the latest_address_book CTE selects a partially-written address book snapshot, causing the API to return a reorganized or incomplete view of the network node set?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a :nodeIds array containing node IDs that existed in a previous address book version but not the current one, and by combining with a specific :fileId, cause the query to return historical node records as if they were current, reorganizing the apparent active node set?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an extremely large :nodeIds array (e.g., thousands of entries) to the findNetworkNodes() call, causing the PostgreSQL IN clause (abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)) to perform a full table scan across address_book_entry, effectively reorganizing query execution order and potentially surfacing cross-version node data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set :minNodeId to 0 and :maxNodeId to Long.MAX_VALUE with an empty :nodeIds array, bypassing the node ID filter entirely (coalesce(array_length(:nodeIds, 1), 0) = 0) and retrieving all nodes from the latest address book, enabling full enumeration of the current network topology without any restriction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the node_info CTE performs an unrestricted SELECT from the node table (no timestamp filter), causing the query to join current node metadata (admin_key, grpc_proxy_endpoint) with a historical address book entry, producing a historically inconsistent and reorganized view of node state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) logic in the SELECT clause to observe that when a node record exists in the node table with a different account_id than the address book entry, the API silently returns the node table value, creating a discrepancy that misrepresents the historical account assignment recorded in the address book?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with :limit set to the maximum allowed value (25) and :orderDirection='DESC' to retrieve the most recently added nodes first, then use the returned startConsensusTimestamp values to reconstruct the chronological order of node additions, effectively mapping the full history of address book reorganizations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ab.end_consensus_timestamp field returned in the response to determine when a specific address book version was superseded, enabling reconstruction of the full timeline of address book transitions and network topology reorganizations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply :minNodeId greater than :maxNodeId to the repository directly (bypassing the service-layer check in NetworkServiceImpl.getNetworkNodes()), causing the WHERE clause (abe.node_id >= :minNodeId AND abe.node_id <= :maxNodeId) to return zero rows, effectively hiding all nodes and presenting an empty network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ab.start_consensus_timestamp returned in the response to correlate address book versions with specific consensus timestamps, enabling the attacker to identify the exact moment a network reorganization (node addition/removal) was committed to the ledger?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user issue repeated paginated requests (using the link.next cursor) with alternating :orderDirection values to observe inconsistent node orderings between pages, causing the pagination to skip or duplicate nodes and presenting a reorganized view of the node list to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the serviceEndpointsJson subquery orders by (abse.ip_address_v4 ASC, abse.port ASC) independently of the outer query's :orderDirection, causing the returned service endpoint list to be in a different order than the node list, creating an inconsistent and potentially misleading view of network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a :fileId value of 0 or a negative number to the findNetworkNodes() query, causing the latest_address_book CTE to return no rows (WHERE file_id = :fileId), and the outer JOIN to produce an empty result set, effectively making the entire network node list disappear from the API response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeCertHash CASE expression (convert_from(abe.node_cert_hash, 'UTF8')) to trigger a PostgreSQL error when node_cert_hash contains non-UTF8 bytes, causing a 500 error that reveals internal database state or disrupts the availability of the node history endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicKey CASE expression (left(abe.public_key, 2) = '0x') to observe that nodes with public keys not prefixed with '0x' have '0x' prepended, while those already prefixed do not, creating an inconsistent encoding that could cause downstream consumers to misidentify node public keys and reorganize their trust model?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.max_stake, -1) and nullif(ns.min_stake, -1) logic to observe that nodes with max_stake or min_stake of exactly -1 are returned as NULL, masking the actual stored value and causing downstream consumers to misinterpret the staking history of those nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LEFT JOIN to latest_node_stake to observe that nodes present in the address book but absent from node_stake return NULL stake values, causing the API to present an incomplete and potentially misleading view of which nodes are actively staking in the current network configuration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LEFT JOIN to node_info to observe that nodes present in the address book but absent from the node table return NULL for admin_key, grpc_proxy_endpoint, and decline_reward, causing the API to present a historically inconsistent view where some nodes appear to have no administrative configuration?
